opp.ais_why:
• Even though Merlin isn't in the explicit product scope and the RFP carries no AI rating, the May 2026 demo captured clear buyer-stated AI enthusiasm. Procurement lead Andrew Simmons praised the agentic negotiation feature as awesome, and Craig Adler asked directly: "Within the workflow, can you deploy an agent?" Those are buyer-initiated agentic questions.
• Craig also described the Merlin Intake use case unprompted: "Can you populate an intake for me and figure out what category I'm buying?" Ida Stark and Maria Murillo backed that up with engagement on Smartdesk invoice automation, and Bailey Erazo committed to share Zylow integration details and ballpark pricing, signalling real evaluation traction.
• Why not Hungry: the explicit in-scope set is iContract, iSource, and iSupplier with no Merlin or ANA module committed, the September go-live is targeted at an MVP NetSuite plus AP integration (not AI), and risks include supplier portal adoption that's unlikely to hit 100%. Buyer enthusiasm is real but not yet anchored to a paid AI workstream.

metrics.narrative: Andy Simmons articulated rich qualitative pain on the 12 Dec intro call but never quantified it: 'we only use NetSuite... shoehorned NetSuite into a procure-to-pay... not a lot of emphasis on sourcing.' Core driver is a 'singular front door' for intake (today via scattered email and Teams) plus standing up a sourcing practice the company does not have at all. No cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings baseline exists, so there is no ROI model behind the $150K — a real exposure now that the PROS acquisition (closed ~1 Feb) puts spend under scrutiny. Move: co-build a quantified case (manual-intake cost, sourcing-savings target, AP cycle-time) Andy can carry to the budget owner.

champion.narrative: Andy is a developing champion who authored and issued the RFP, invited Zycus in writing, shaped criteria around Zycus strengths (full suite, Teams UI, AI negotiation), and brought procurement, AP, and IT to the demo. What is missing and now at risk: he has gone quiet since 30 May, never confirmed the integration call or the live NetSuite offer, and never introduced the economic buyer. His advocacy is real but unproven at the power level. Because he owns the spec, he is the path to the budget owner — re-arm him, do not route around him.

competition.narrative: Andy said Zycus is 'amongst a few other players' but never named the shortlist. The clearest current rival is the status quo — NetSuite-only manual P2P — which becomes the realistic outcome if post-acquisition budget tightens. Coupa and Zip surface only from Andy's own usage history (Coupa at prior roles, Zip at Procore), not as confirmed bidders; the Ivalua/Coupa sighting on 17 Mar belongs to a different (BakerRipley) RFP and is not weighted. Wedge: Zycus is the only full intake-to-pay suite with native Teams UI and direct vendor-delivered implementation — fitting Conga's IT staffing gap. Proving NetSuite integration depth live is how we convert the wedge.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is concrete and owned by Andy, the new Sr Director of Procurement hired to modernize the function: NetSuite shoehorned into P2P with no real sourcing; chaotic intake via 'email, Teams, or just meetings'; need for 'one singular front door... normalized data flowing through.' AP intake is manual drag-and-drop into MineralTree (Ida Stark, 6 May). Compounding constraint: 'our IT team went through a massive rift earlier this year,' so Conga needs Zycus to own deployment. Post-PROS scaling magnifies the manual-process strain.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early. Mutual NDA signed in January. Implementation is Zycus-delivered, not partner-led ('Zycus implements Zycus') — a fit given Conga's IT rift. A phased 12-20 week plan was agreed on 6 May, but the buyer never returned the user-count input form needed to firm pricing, so no quote, MSA/Order Form, legal, or InfoSec review has started. The path to paper runs through pricing finalization and the unscheduled integration scoping call — both currently stalled on the buyer.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer mapped or engaged in 6.5 months. Andy said on 12 Dec he has 'a lot of people behind this up to the C-level' and 'I'm not doing this on my own,' and the 6 May demo confirmed a CFO sits in the approval chain ('anything above $1,000 goes to you and the CFO'), but that person is unnamed and unengaged. The 26 Jun outreach to Nicole Gallego (SVP Delivery Ops & IT) is the first reach toward a more senior buyer. Because Andy authored the RFP, the path to power runs THROUGH him (or through Nicole if she engages) — broker the introduction, do not cold-outreach a CFO. This is the single highest-leverage gap and the reason Best Case cannot stand.

decision_process.narrative: Process is readable: NDA signed (Jan), 45-question RFP issued 13 Apr with a concurrent-demo format, response submitted ~29 Apr, demo 6 May, integration/implementation scoping call set as the next gate — and never booked. Andy targeted a March decision tied to the PROS close (~1 Feb); that date has slipped with no selection by late June. The 6 May demo widened the committee (procurement, AP, IT), but the approval chain above Andy and any PROS-parent governance gate remain unconfirmed. The unbooked integration call is the gating step between here and selection.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from the RFP and demo. Andy required the full source-to-pay suite except CLM (Conga keeps its own): 'I really need the whole thing to layer over... the only exception is CLM.' Must-haves: single intake front door, normalized data flow, automatic PR/PO creation and backend invoicing, an esourcing practice, AI/automated negotiation, spend analytics, and two-way API integrations to NetSuite, Conga CLM, ZenGRC, and Microsoft Teams (Teams as primary UI). The 45-question RFP with an accelerated window confirms a structured, criteria-led evaluation. NetSuite integration depth is the live battleground criterion.

hard.ais_why:
• Even though Merlin isn't in the explicit product scope and the RFP carries no AI rating, the May 2026 demo captured clear buyer-stated AI enthusiasm. Procurement lead Andrew Simmons praised the agentic negotiation feature as awesome, and Craig Adler asked directly: "Within the workflow, can you deploy an agent?" Those are buyer-initiated agentic questions.
• Craig also described the Merlin Intake use case unprompted: "Can you populate an intake for me and figure out what category I'm buying?" Ida Stark and Maria Murillo backed that up with engagement on Smartdesk invoice automation, and Bailey Erazo committed to share Zylow integration details and ballpark pricing, signalling real evaluation traction.
• Why not Hungry: the explicit in-scope set is iContract, iSource, and iSupplier with no Merlin or ANA module committed, the September go-live is targeted at an MVP NetSuite plus AP integration (not AI), and risks include supplier portal adoption that's unlikely to hit 100%. Buyer enthusiasm is real but not yet anchored to a paid AI workstream.
